semanticVisualQuery: Living World and Earth Systems Overview

assetDebugEntries.effectiveSearchQuery: living world and earth systems overview
assetDebugEntries.resolvedAltText: Listing of essential considerations for the sustainable exploration and utilization of sensitive celestial bodies in the solar system. Large populations of living organisms may already be thriving on ocean worlds like Europa, Enceladus, Ganymede, Callisto or Titan. Forward contamination should be prevented by sterilizing space probes sent to sensitive areas of the Solar System.The Committee on Space Research (COSPAR) presents recommendations for avoiding interplanetary contamination depending on the type of space mission and the celestial body explored. For both landers and orbiters, spacecraft trajectories should be designed so that if communications are lost, they will miss the target to prevent unintended impacts.
